postData.contentHtml:
Residents across the northeastern United States were recently treated to an unexpected celestial spectacle and a powerful auditory experience. A massive fireball meteor, estimated to be about 5 feet wide, exploded high above the East Coast, generating a sonic boom that rattled homes and sent shockwaves through communities, particularly in Boston, Rhode Island, and parts of New York.

NASA's initial analysis confirmed the event, revealing that the meteor detonated with an astonishing force equivalent to approximately 230 to 300 tons of TNT. Traveling at a blistering speed of 75,000 miles per hour, the meteor broke apart at an altitude of 40 miles, creating the thunderous boom that many mistook for an earthquake or other seismic activity.

Reports of sightings poured in from various locations, describing a bright object streaking across the sky before the ground-shaking boom. Social media was abuzz with discussions, ranging from awe and wonder to theories about the mysterious noise. While the event sparked considerable debate online, the overall sentiment leaned towards fascination with this rare natural phenomenon.

This incident serves as a powerful reminder of the dynamic nature of our solar system and the incredible forces at play even far above our heads.
postData.title: East Coast Rattled by Massive Meteor Explosion and Sonic Boom
postData.summary: A colossal meteor recently disintegrated over the northeastern United States, unleashing a powerful sonic boom that shook homes and startled residents across the East Coast, from Massachusetts to New York. NASA confirmed the event, detailing the immense energy released by the celestial visitor.
